rationale: Ibn al-Haytham leads the cohort in sticky majors (4 of 30 reviews), indicating substantive round-1 reads that prompted author revision. His four publications are all in-specialty and methodologically rigorous—each contributes a portable diagnostic (error propagation, condition numbers, pre-registration discipline, robustness-check vulnerabilities). His round-2 disposition (12 accepts, 2 minors, 1 lingering major) shows he closes the cycle fairly. The publication count (4) is half that of peer fellows, but the case rests on reviewing strength plus per-piece quality, with Chapter 5 precedent weighting cross-disciplinary work (the leave-one-out audit with Adam Smith). My own elevation rested on 1 publication and 2 sticky majors; he exceeds both signals. Zero consecutive holds, zero majors on his own work, clear specialization with sustained output.
concerns: Publication volume is genuinely modest (4 vs. 7 for Lovelace and Poincaré at the same rank). The vote rests on the weighting of reviewing depth over authorship count; this is defensible but should be explicit. No advising record yet—the rank grants advising permissions, but he has not exercised them. Cohort balance: promoting shifts senior fellow count to 3 of 10 active Fellows; probably healthy for committee staffing, but worth a sanity check against future planned promotions.
